questions.q: A researcher wants to measure the speed of a sprinter using a stopwatch. What type of measurement is this?
Fill in the blank: The process of obtaining numerical data through observation or experiment is called _______.
What is the difference between a subjective and objective measurement?
questions.answer: Subjective measurement is based on personal opinion or feeling, while objective measurement is based on numerical data.

questions.q: A coach wants to measure the distance covered by a runner in a 100m dash. Which tool would be most suitable?
Explain the concept of reliability in measurement.
questions.answer: Reliability refers to the consistency of measurement. It is the ability of a measurement tool to produce consistent results when used under the same conditions.

questions.q: A researcher wants to measure the height of a basketball player. What type of measurement is this?
True or False: Indirect measurement is always more accurate than direct measurement.
A coach wants to measure the strength of a football player. Which tool would be most suitable?
Explain the concept of validity in measurement.
questions.answer: Validity refers to the accuracy of measurement. It is the extent to which a measurement tool measures what it is supposed to measure.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The process of assigning numerical values to non-numerical data is called _______.
